# Portal

A utility component that renders its children into a DOM node outside the parent component hierarchy. Useful for rendering modals, tooltips, and dropdowns that need to escape overflow or z-index constraints.

## Import

```jsx
import { Portal } from "@onlinesales-ai/ui";
```

## Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `container` | `HTMLElement \| RefObject<HTMLElement>` | `document.body` | The DOM node to render the children into. |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | `false` | When `true`, children are rendered in place without portalling. |

## Usage

### Basic Portal

```jsx
<Portal>
  <div>This content is rendered at the end of document.body.</div>
</Portal>
```

### Portal to Custom Container

```jsx
function CustomPortal() {
  const containerRef = useRef(null);

  return (
    <>
      <div ref={containerRef} />
      <Portal container={containerRef}>
        <div>Rendered inside the container div above.</div>
      </Portal>
    </>
  );
}
```

### Disabled Portal

```jsx
<Portal disabled>
  <div>This renders in place, not in a portal.</div>
</Portal>
```
